Get-Date.description: Gets the current date and time or a specified date and time.

Get-Help.description: Displays information about PowerShell commands and concepts.

Get-EventLog.description: Gets events from event logs on the local or remote computers.

Get-WinEvent.description: Gets events from event logs and event tracing log files.

Get-ComputerInfo.description: Gets a consolidated object of system and operating system properties.

Test-Connection.description: Sends ICMP echo request packets to test network connectivity.

Test-Path.description: Determines whether all elements of a path exist.

Get-NetAdapter.description: Gets the basic network adapter properties.

Restart-Service.description: Stops and then starts one or more services.

Restart-Computer.description: Restarts the operating system on local and remote computers.

Stop-Service.description: Stops one or more running services.

Get-ExecutionPolicy.description: Gets the execution policies for the current session.

Get-Random.description: Gets a random number or selects objects randomly from a collection.

Select-String.description: Searches for text patterns in strings and files.

ConvertTo-Json.description: Converts an object to a JSON-formatted string.

ConvertFrom-Json.description: Converts a JSON-formatted string to a custom object.

Get-CimInstance.description: Gets CIM instances from a CIM server.

Get-ADUser.description: Gets one or more Active Directory users.

Get-ADGroup.description: Gets one or more Active Directory groups.

%.description: Performs an operation against each item in a collection of input objects.

?.description: Selects objects from a collection based on their property values.

ac.description: Appends content, such as words or data, to a file.

asnp.description: Adds one or more Windows PowerShell snap-ins to the current session.

cat.description: Gets the contents of a file.

cd.description: Sets the current working location to a specified location.

chdir.description: Sets the current working location to a specified location.

clc.description: Deletes the contents of an item, but does not delete the item.

clear.description: Clears the display in the host program.

clhy.description: Deletes entries from the command history.

cli.description: Deletes the contents of an item, but does not delete the item.

clp.description: Deletes the value of a property but does not delete the property.

cls.description: Clears the display in the host program.

clv.description: Deletes the value of a variable.

copy.description: Copies an item from one location to another.

cp.description: Copies an item from one location to another.

cpi.description: Copies an item from one location to another.

cpp.description: Copies a property and value from a specified location to another location.

curl.description: Gets content from a webpage on the Internet.

cvpa.description: Converts a path from a Windows PowerShell path to a Windows PowerShell provider path.

dbp.description: Disables the breakpoints in the current console.

dir.description: Gets the files and folders in a file system drive.

ebp.description: Enables the breakpoints in the current console.

echo.description: Sends the specified objects to the next command in the pipeline. If the command is the last command in the pipeline, the objects are displayed in the console.

epal.description: Exports information about currently defined aliases to a file.

epcsv.description: Converts objects into a series of comma-separated (CSV) strings and saves the strings in a CSV file.

epsn.description: Imports commands from another session and saves them in a Windows PowerShell module.

etsn.description: Starts an interactive session with a remote computer.

exsn.description: Ends an interactive session with a remote computer.

fc.description: Uses a customized view to format the output.

fl.description: Formats the output as a list of properties in which each property appears on a new line.

foreach.description: Performs an operation against each item in a collection of input objects.

ft.description: Formats the output as a table.

fw.description: Formats objects as a wide table that displays only one property of each object.

gal.description: Gets the aliases for the current session.

gbp.description: Gets the breakpoints that are set in the current session.

gc.description: Gets the contents of a file.

gci.description: Gets the files and folders in a file system drive.

gdr.description: Gets drives in the current session.

ghy.description: Gets a list of the commands entered during the current session.

gjb.description: Gets Windows PowerShell background jobs that are running in the current session.

gl.description: Gets information about the current working location or a location stack.

gm.description: Gets the properties and methods of objects.

gmo.description: Gets the modules that have been imported or that can be imported into the current session.

gp.description: Gets the properties of a specified item.

gps.description: Gets the processes that are running on the local computer or a remote computer.

group.description: Groups objects that contain the same value for specified properties.

gsn.description: Gets the Windows PowerShell sessions on local and remote computers.

gsnp.description: Gets the Windows PowerShell snap-ins on the computer.

gsv.description: Gets the services on a local or remote computer.

gu.description: Returns unique items from a sorted list.

gv.description: Gets the variables in the current console.

gwmi.description: Gets instances of Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) classes or information about the available classes.

h.description: Gets a list of the commands entered during the current session.

history.description: Gets a list of the commands entered during the current session.

icm.description: Runs commands on local and remote computers.

iex.description: Runs commands or expressions on the local computer.

ihy.description: Runs commands from the session history.

ii.description: Performs the default action on the specified item.

ipal.description: Imports an alias list from a file.

ipcsv.description: Creates table-like custom objects from the items in a CSV file.

ipmo.description: Adds modules to the current session.

ipsn.description: Imports commands from another session into the current session.

irm.description: Sends an HTTP or HTTPS request to a RESTful web service.

ise.description: Explains how to use the PowerShell_ISE.exe command-line tool.

iwr.description: Gets content from a web page on the Internet.

kill.description: Stops one or more running processes.

ls.description: Gets the files and folders in a file system drive.

man.description: Displays information about Windows PowerShell commands and concepts.

measure.description: Calculates the numeric properties of objects, and the characters, words, and lines in string objects, such as files of text.

mi.description: Moves an item from one location to another.

mount.description: Creates temporary and persistent mapped network drives.

move.description: Moves an item from one location to another.

mp.description: Moves a property from one location to another.

mv.description: Moves an item from one location to another.

ndr.description: Creates temporary and persistent mapped network drives.

nmo.description: Creates a new dynamic module that exists only in memory.

npssc.description: Creates a file that defines a session configuration.

nsn.description: Creates a persistent connection to a local or remote computer.

ogv.description: Sends output to an interactive table in a separate window.

oh.description: Sends output to the command line.

popd.description: Changes the current location to the location most recently pushed to the stack. You can pop the location from the default stack or from a stack that you create by using the Push-Location cmdlet.

ps.description: Gets the processes that are running on the local computer or a remote computer.

pushd.description: Adds the current location to the top of a location stack.

pwd.description: Gets information about the current working location or a location stack.

r.description: Runs commands from the session history.

rbp.description: Deletes breakpoints from the current console.

rcjb.description: Gets the results of the Windows PowerShell background jobs in the current session.

rcsn.description: Gets results of commands in disconnected sessions.

rdr.description: Deletes temporary Windows PowerShell drives and disconnects mapped network drives.

ren.description: Renames an item in a Windows PowerShell provider namespace.

rjb.description: Deletes a Windows PowerShell background job.

rmo.description: Removes modules from the current session.

rni.description: Renames an item in a Windows PowerShell provider namespace.

rnp.description: Renames a property of an item.

rp.description: Deletes the property and its value from an item.

rsn.description: Closes one or more Windows PowerShell sessions (PSSessions).

rsnp.description: Removes Windows PowerShell snap-ins from the current session.

rv.description: Deletes a variable and its value.

rvpa.description: Resolves the wildcard characters in a path, and displays the path contents.

rwmi.description: Deletes an instance of an existing Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) class.

sajb.description: Starts a Windows PowerShell background job.

sal.description: Creates or changes an alias (alternate name) for a cmdlet or other command element in the current Windows PowerShell session.

saps.description: Starts one or more processes on the local computer.

sasv.description: Starts one or more stopped services.

sbp.description: Sets a breakpoint on a line, command, or variable.

sc.description: Replaces the contents of a file with contents that you specify.

set.description: Sets the value of a variable. Creates the variable if one with the requested name does not exist.

shcm.description: Creates Windows PowerShell commands in a graphical command window.

si.description: Changes the value of an item to the valu

Measure-Object.description: Calculates aggregate statistics such as count, sum, average, minimum, and maximum for object properties.

Group-Object.description: Groups objects that have the same value for a specified property and provides counts for each group.

Select-Object.description: Selects specified properties of an object or a set of objects.

Get-Unique.description: Returns unique items from a sorted list.

Select-Object-ExpandProperty.description: Extracts the value of a property instead of returning an object containing that property.

Where-Object.description: Filters objects based on property values.

ForEach-Object.description: Performs an operation on each object in a pipeline.

Get-Command.description: Gets commands, cmdlets, functions, aliases, and applications available in the session.

New-Item.description: Creates a new item such as a file, directory, registry key, or other provider item.

Remove-Item.description: Deletes files, folders, registry keys, and other provider items.

Rename-Item.description: Changes the name of an item.

Get-Member.description: Gets the properties and methods of objects.
